judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation with correct file/line citations and equivalent fixes; they are comparable, with Report 1 marginally more explicit on removing the shadow sink and confirming migrations.

report_1.evidence: infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2 — ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Remove the key entirely from the prod tfvars ... infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4 — PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin": ... Remove the key entirely ... Both keys must be removed entirely so shadow_scoring_sink is "" ... LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ... It should be omitted from prod

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2 — ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true". ... change to "false" (or remove the key ...) ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin". ... set to empty / remove. ... Either empty the sink to genuinely disable the feature in prod, or point it at a dedicated prod path ... Leaving the staging/ path is wrong in every scenario.

report_1.evidence: deploy-prod deploys the same already-built digest to prod ... production ships the staging-mode artifact, and the API base URL baked into the shipped production bundle is https://staging-api.example.com ... Production needs its own build with a production env file

report_2.evidence: the production frontend bundle will contain the hardcoded staging API URL https://staging-api.example.com baked into its JavaScript ... Fixing this requires either a separate production build (--mode production plus a new .env.production) with a second image/digest, or moving the API base URL to a runtime-injected configuration

report_1.evidence: No database migration step. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but the runbook is self-declared stale ... nothing in deploy.yml runs the three migrations ... the full migration chain needs an explicit (likely manual) first pass — "pipeline succeeded" would not mean the schema exists.

report_2.evidence: No migration step exists. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in the workflow runs the three migrations ... For a first production release, the entire schema needs to be created; there is currently no mechanism for that. The runbook is also self-dated ... so its claims can't be trusted

report_1.evidence: Production (as committed): infra/prod/main.tf:3 sets the same staging condition — repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging. ... The condition must be changed to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production — a production role needs its own scoped trust condition, never one shared with staging.

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 — an unedited copy from staging — configures the production deploy role to trust repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging instead. ... The fix is to parameterize the role name and set the prod condition to ...:environment:production.
